value: In 2020, Baystate Super Health established goals to increase local hiring between five to ten percent annually. Samra and Pacheco collaborated with Dr. Frank Robinson, vice president of public health and community relations at Baystate Health, to identify specific census codes to focus on (rather than zip codes, which they found to be too nebulous). “We had been struggling the most with identifying the underesourced neighborhoods that we want to be targeting,” Samra admitted. “That’s helping us really stay factbased around what’s happening in our organization,” highlighted Samra. These local hiring goals were set at the executive level and linked with a leadership bonus incentive program. “There’s some skin in the game in terms of achievement,” stated Samra.
